title: Raids on Pinarayi Vijayan: ED gives CPM rallying point to unite divided cadres
content: Kannur: The Directorate of Enforcement (ED) raids linked to the Exalogic Consulting-Cochin Minerals and Rutile Limited (CMRL) deal appear to have handed the CPM leadership an opportunity to rally a deeply divided organisation after its crushing defeat in the Assembly election. The leadership, including Pinarayi Vijayan and party secretary M V Govindan, had been facing mounting anger from the cadre after the election debacle. But now they have come forward describing the ED raids as an attack by the BJP-led Union government, and also actively encouraged by the Congress-led UDF government in the state. Pinarayi led the charge. He came out to address the media and cadres violently protesting outside his rented house at Bakery Junction in Thiruvananthapuram. Whenever the enemies of the party tried to prick (us), the party has always extended its wholehearted support. Today, party comrades have once again made it clear that there is no weakening or wavering of that support, he said. I congratulate all of you once again. We can face anything. That is our strength. Let us come out together. When the ED officials and their CRPF security personnel left Vijayan's rented house in Thiruvananthapuram on Wednesday evening, suspected CPM workers attacked the convoy, smashing windshields with bricks. Vijayan did not refer to the violence while addressing the media after the searches. Instead, he accused the Congress and Rahul Gandhi of politically encouraging the ED action. On Wednesday, standing outside his rented house, Vijayan said: What was on display today was the emotion of the state and the emotions of the party comrades. Vijayan, ignoring the violent attack on the ED vehicles but calling the party workers' actions as emotions, was reminiscent of him describing the brutal assaults on Youth Congress workers in Kannur and Alappuzha by his gunman, police officers and DYFI activists as a rescue operation during the Nava Kerala Sadas campaign in 2023, when he was the chief minister. However, the party leadership is hoping to ride on this emotion to bridge the chasm that has deepened within the organisation after severe setbacks in the polls, including in traditional strongholds such as Kannur, where sections of CPM cadres themselves turned against the leadership. Lower-level committees openly demanded leadership changes and course correction after the defeat. Vijayan himself was less popular in his Dharmadam constituency, as he trailed in the first six rounds of counting before eventually winning by 19,247 votes, a sharp fall from his 50,123-vote margin in 2021. Party secretary M V Govindan's wife, P K Shyamala, was defeated in Taliparamba, another CPM bastion held by him. Now, the CPM leadership is attempting to frame the ED action as a joint political operation by the BJP-led Union government and the Congress-led UDF government in Kerala, because in Kerala, the UDF is its primary electoral rival. The BJP has only three MLAs. The beleaguered LDF has been reduced to 35 MLAs in the 140-member Assembly. The effect of that call for unity was also visible outside Vijayan's house at Pandyalamukku in Pinarayi village, where CPM workers and supporters gathered in large numbers from Wednesday morning. Mid-level leaders addressed workers seated in circles outside the house as slogans were raised calling on cadres to unite and make the party stronger. ED officials had been searching the house since 5.45 am. Speaking in Thiruvananthapuram, Vijayan said, I think this inspection will give mental satisfaction to some people, particularly to a person like Rahul Gandhi. Rahul Gandhi has repeatedly asked why Pinarayi Vijayan's house is not being raided and why Pinarayi Vijayan is not being arrested. In our country, the BJP government has consistently pursued calculated actions against the Opposition and Opposition leaders. Strong protests have emerged across the country against such actions...
